meaning: n.解释者,说明者,代表者,典型,指数
examples: She was a leading exponent of free trade during her political career.
The Alvin Ailey American Dance Theatre was formed in the 1950s and quickly established itself as a leading exponent of progressive choreography and contemporary dance.
But the best exponent of Republican isolationism is surely the televisually expert Pat Buchanan.
Amr Moussa, the secretary-general of the Arab League, is an arch-exponent of this conditioned response.
